mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-decline months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession expectations that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings revisions upward and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or forward returns are negative in more than 55% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 2.5 percent forces the Federal Reserve to prioritize price stability over growth, triggering equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal reverses direction during disinflation periods without regime adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect accelerating manufacturing output and supply-side momentum, which generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with INDPRO changes, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payrolls decline exceeding 50,000 signals abrupt labor market contraction that undermines consumer spending forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of months with payroll drops above 50,000, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and monthly retail sales growth validate sustained aggregate demand, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 50% of co-expansion quarters, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during late-cycle expansions indicates capacity utilization exhaustion and impending economic slowdown, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate in more than 60% of late-cycle production declines, or the mechanism fails to distinguish late-cycle from early-cycle dynamics in walk-forward tests.
